title: Finding probability from a simple sample space
problem: A spinner has $8$ equal sections numbered $1$ to $8$. It is spun once. Find the probability of landing on a number less than $4$.

steps.working: The spinner can land on any of the numbers $1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8$. There are $8$ equally likely outcomes.
Numbers less than $4$: $\{1, 2, 3\}$. There are $3$ favourable outcomes.

steps.instruction: Write the probability as a fraction

title: Probability of a combined event
problem: A fair six-sided die is rolled. Find the probability of rolling an odd number or a number greater than $4$.

steps.instruction: List the outcomes for each condition
Find the union (all outcomes satisfying either condition)
steps.working: Union: $\{1, 3, 5\} \cup \{5, 6\} = \{1, 3, 5, 6\}$. Note that $5$ appears in both sets but is only counted once. There are $4$ favourable outcomes.

title: Probability using inclusion–exclusion
problem: A number is chosen at random from the integers $1$ to $20$ inclusive. Find the probability that it is a multiple of $2$ or a multiple of $3$.

steps.instruction: Find $P(A \cap B)$: multiples of both $2$ and $3$ (i.e. multiples of $6$)
steps.working: Multiples of $6$: $6, 12, 18$ — that is $3$ numbers. $P(A \cap B) = \dfrac{3}{20}$.
